items.title: The Budget (Scotland) Act 2002 Amendment Order 2003
items.description: This Order amends the Budget (Scotland) Act 2002 (“the 2002 Act”), which makes provision, for financial year 2002/03, for the use of resources by the Scottish Administration and certain bodies whose expenditure is payable out of the Scottish Consolidated Fund, for authorising the use of resources, and for limits on the capital expenditure of and borrowing of local authorities and certain other public bodies. In particular, the Order amends that Act so as to–

items.title: The National Health Service (Dental Charges) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations consolidate, with a number of minor drafting amendments, the provisions of the National Health Service (Dental Charges) (Scotland) Regulations 1989 (“the 1989 Regulations”). The 1989 Regulations and all subsequent amendments are revoked by regulation 14 and Schedule 4 of these Regulations.

items.title: The National Health Service (Functions of the Common Services Agency) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2003
items.description: This Order amends the National Health Service (Functions of the Common Services Agency) (Scotland) Order 1974 (“the principal Order”).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates (Levying) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to the amount payable in certain circumstances as non-domestic rates in respect of property in Scotland. They apply only to financial year 2003-2004 (“the relevant year”).

items.title: The Housing Support Grant (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order fixes for the year 2003 2004 the aggregate amount of the housing support grants payable to some local authorities under section 191 of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1987; lists the councils among whom the grants will be apportioned; and prescribes the method of apportionment among those councils of the general and hostel portions of the aggregate amount.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Solicitors in the Sheriff Court) (Amendment) 2003
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Table of Fees in the Schedule to the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Solicitors in the Sheriff Court) (Amendment and Further Provisions) 1993 (S.I. 1993/30080) by increasing the fees payable to solicitors by about 12%. The last increase to the fees mentioned in this Act of Sederunt was in the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Solicitors in the Sheriff Court) (Amendment) 2002 (S.S.I. 2002/274). The Act of Sederunt (Fees of Solicitors in the Sheriff Court) (Amendment No. 3) 2002 (S.S.I. 2002/328) substituted the Table of Fees in the Schedule to that instrument for the Table of Fees in the Schedule to S.S.I. 2002/274.

items.title: The Advice and Assistance (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Advice and Assistance (Scotland) Regulations 1996 to provide that–

items.title: The Common Agricultural Policy (Wine) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Scotland only, amend the Common Agricultural Policy (Wine) (Scotland) Regulations 2002 (S.S.I. 2002/325) which provide for the enforcement of EC Regulations concerned with the production and marketing of wine and related products.

items.title: The Products of Animal Origin (Third Country Imports) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, amend the Products of Animal Origin (Third Country Imports) (Scotland) Regulations 2002 (S.S.I. 2002/445) (“the principal Regulations”) as follows:

items.title: The Prohibition of Fishing with Multiple Trawls (No. 2) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2003
items.description: This Order amends the Prohibition of Fishing with Multiple Trawls (No. 2) (Scotland) Order 2000 (S.S.I. 2000/405) (“the principal Order”). The Order forms part of the law of Scotland only.

items.title: The Sea Fish (Specified Sea Areas) (Regulation of Nets and Other Fishing Gear) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2003
items.description: This Order amends the Sea Fish (Specified Sea Areas) (Regulation of Nets and Other Fishing Gear) (Scotland) Order 2000 as amended (S.S.I. 2000/227) (“the principal Order”). This Order like the principal Order is made pursuant to Article 46 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 850/98 of 30th March 1998 for the conservation of fishery resources through technical measures for the protection of juveniles of marine organisms (O.J. No. L 125, 27.4.98, p.1). This Order forms part of the law of Scotland only.

items.title: The Anti-Pollution Works (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, prescribe the contents of anti-pollution works notices served under section 46A of the Control of Pollution Act 1974 (“the 1974 Act”), the procedure to be followed in relation to appeals to the Scottish Ministers against such notices and the procedures relating to compensation for rights of entry in connection with anti-pollution works paid under section 46B of that Act (regulations 3 to 8 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Action Programme for Nitrate Vulnerable Zones (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Action Programme for Nitrate Vulnerable Zones (Scotland) Regulations 2003. In particular, they amend those Regulations so as to–

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 6) (Scotland) Order 2002 Revocation Order 2003
items.description: This version of the The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 6) (Scotland) Order 2002 Revocation Order 2003 is the published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer for Scotland. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Waste Incineration (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations are made under section 2 of the Pollution Prevention and Control Act 1999 (c. 24.). They implement in Scotland the provisions of Directive 2000/76/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 4 December 2000 on the incineration of waste (O.J. No. L 332, 28.12.00, p.91.) (the Directive), together with the Pollution Prevention and Control (Waste Incineration Directive) (Scotland) Directions 2003 and the Environmental Protection Act (Waste Incineration Directive) (Scotland) Directions 2003.

items.title: The Waste Management Licensing Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations make miscellaneous changes to waste management legislation by amending the Environment Act 1995 (c. 25) (“the 1995 Act”) and the Waste Management Licensing Regulations 1994 (“the 1994 Regulations”).

items.title: The Police Grant (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order is made under section 32(3) and (5) of the Police (Scotland) Act 1967, as substituted by section 45(1) of the Crime and Punishment (Scotland) Act 1997, which makes provision for the Scottish Ministers to make grants out of money from the Scottish Consolidated Fund for police purposes to police authorities and joint police boards.

items.title: The Financial Assistance for Environmental Purposes (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order varies section 153(1) of the Environmental Protection Act 1990 so as to enable the Scottish Ministers to give financial assistance to, or for the purposes of, the Waste Minimisation and Recycling Programme, the Carbon Trust and for any scheme for the regulation of emissions from motor vehicles.

items.title: The Zoo Licensing Act 1981 Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, contain provisions implementing Council Directive 1999/22/EC relating to the keeping of wild animals in zoos (O.J. No. L 094, 9.4.1999 p.24 26) (“the Directive”). For this purpose they amend the Zoo Licensing Act 1981 (c. 37) (“the Act”) as follows.

items.title: The Planning and Compensation Act 1991 (Amendment of Schedule 18) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: Part I of Schedule 18 to the Planning and Compensation Act 1991 specifies or describes the date from which interest is payable on compensation arising under the statutory provisions mentioned, which do not themselves provide for the payment of interest.

items.title: The Council Tax (Discounts) (Scotland) Consolidation and Amendment Order 2003
items.description: This Order consolidates with amendments and revokes the provisions of the Council Tax (Discounts) (Scotland) Order 1992 (“the 1992 Order”) and Orders amending that Order.

items.title: The Rural Stewardship Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Rural Stewardship Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2001 (“the principal Regulations”), which implemented Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1750/1999 (O.J. No. L 214, 13.8.99, p.31) on support for rural development from the European Agricultural Guidance and Guarantee Fund.

items.title: The Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Regulations 1989 (“the 1989 Regulations”) to provide a new scheme for the payment of civil legal aid fees to solicitors.

items.title: The Advice and Assistance (Assistance by Way of Representation) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments and revoke the Advice and Assistance (Assistance by Way of Representation) (Scotland) Regulations 1997 and subsequent amending instruments.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 15) (Scotland) Order 2002 Revocation Order 2003
items.description: This version of the The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 15) (Scotland) Order 2002 Revocation Order 2003 is the published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer for Scotland. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Advice and Assistance (Financial Conditions) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations, in relation to any case where an application for advice and assistance is made on or after 7th April 2003–

items.title: The Regulation of Investigatory Powers (Covert Human Intelligence Sources – Code of Practice) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order brings into force the code of practice issued under section 24 of the Regulation of Investigatory Powers (Scotland) Act 2000 relating to covert human intelligence sources. A person exercising or performing a power or duty to which the code applies shall, in doing so, have regard to the provisions of the code in so far as they are applicable.

items.title: The Civil Legal Aid (Financial Conditions) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations increase certain of the financial limits of eligibility for civil legal aid under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1986.

items.title: The Regulation of Investigatory Powers (Covert Surveillance – Code of Practice) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order brings into force the code of practice issued under section 24 of the Regulation of Investigatory Powers (Scotland) Act 2000 relating to covert surveillance. A person exercising or performing a power or duty to which the code applies shall, in doing so, have regard to the provisions of the code in so far as they are applicable.

items.title: The Members of the Parole Board (Removal Tribunal) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the procedure to be followed by and before a tribunal constituted by and under paragraph 3B of Schedule 2 to the Prisoners and Criminal Proceedings (Scotland) Act 1993. Such a tribunal is constituted to carry out an investigation at the request of the Scottish Ministers in order to ascertain whether a member of the Parole Board for Scotland is unfit for office by reason of inability, neglect of duty or misbehaviour.

items.title: The Pollution Prevention and Control (Designation of Landfill Directive) (Scotland) Order 2003 (revoked)
items.description: This Order designates Council Directive 99/31/EC on the landfill of waste (“the Landfill Directive”) as a relevant directive for the purposes of paragraph 20(2)(c) of Schedule 1 to the Pollution Prevention and Control Act 1999 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Water Undertakings (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order makes provision for the valuation for financial years 2002 03 to 2004 05 of certain lands and heritages occupied by Scottish Water and wholly or mainly used for the purposes of water undertakings carried on by that company (“the prescribed class of lands and heritages”) (article 3).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Petrol Filling Stations, Public Houses and Hotels) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: Schedule 2, paragraph 3 to the Local Government and Rating Act 1997 makes provision for mandatory relief from non-domestic rates for certain lands and heritages in rural settlements. Paragraph 3(2)(c)(ii) of that Schedule allows the Scottish Ministers to prescribe conditions by order and if these are satisfied, along with the other requirements of sub-paragraph (2), then the land and heritages will qualify for mandatory relief.

items.title: The Borders and Dumfries and Galloway National Health Service Trusts (Dissolution) Order 2003
items.description: This Order provides by article 2 for the dissolution on 1st April 2003 of the four National Health Service Trusts detailed in Schedule 1 and as a consequence of that revokes the Orders establishing those Trusts and Orders amending those establishment Orders which are detailed in Schedule 2.

items.title: The Intercountry Adoption (Hague Convention) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations implement the 1993 Hague Convention on the Protection of Children and Co operation in respect of Intercountry Adoption that was concluded at The Hague on 29th May 1993.

items.title: The Shetland Islands Council (Papa Stour and Fetlar) Harbour Revision Order 2003
items.description: Shetland Islands Council exercise jurisdiction as harbour authority under the Zetland County Council Act 1974 in respect of the areas specified in Schedule 1 to that Act.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment) (Fees of Solicitors) 2003
items.description: This Act of Sederunt increases certain fees payable to solicitors in the Court of Session under Chapters I and III of the Table of Fees by 12.245%. The following fees in Chapter III are increased by an additional 8.75%: block fees in undefended consistorial actions in Part III, fees for work up to and included in lodging an opposed Outer House petition under Part IV, fees in defended actions under Part V and certain fees for Inner House business under Part VI. The Act also inserts a new Part VA into Chapter III of the Table of Fees which sets out fees for defended personal injury actions commenced on or after 1st April 2003.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 16) (Scotland) Order 2002 Revocation Order 2003
items.description: This Order revokes the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 16) (Scotland) Order 2002 (“the principal Order”) which prohibited–

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (Orkney) (No. 3) (Scotland) Order 2002 Revocation Order 2003
items.description: This Order revokes the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (Orkney) (No. 3) (Scotland) Order 2002 (“the principal Order”) which prohibited–

items.title: The TSE (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description:
These Regulations amend the TSE (Scotland) Regulations 2002 (S.S.I. 2002/255)

				(“the 2002 Regulations”). The majority of the amendments are made in consequence of defects in those Regulations.

items.title: The Ethical Standards in Public Life etc. (Scotland) Act 2000 (Modification of Enactments) Order 2003
items.description: This Order modifies existing primary and subordinate legislation relating to local authorities and public bodies that are subject to the jurisdiction of the Standards Commission in terms of the Ethical Standards in Public Life etc. (Scotland) Act 2000 (“the 2000 Act”).

items.title: The Cairngorms National Park Elections (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order sets out the arrangements under which direct elections to the Cairngorms National Park Authority shall be conducted.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Ophthalmic Services) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Ophthalmic Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”), which provide for arrangements under which ophthalmic medical practitioners and ophthalmic opticians provide general ophthalmic services under the National Health Service.

items.title: The Sheep Scab (Shetland Islands) Order 2003
items.description: This Order enables Shetland Islands Council to make regulations for the purposes of inspecting and testing sheep for the presence of sheep scab mites, immediately on their arrival in the Shetland Islands from another part of the United Kingdom (where those sheep were not previously imported for transit to the Shetland Islands); to require advance notice of movements of sheep to the Islands; and to permit a delay in the mixing of those sheep with other sheep in the Islands, for a maximum period of 48 hours.

items.title: The Ethical Standards in Public Life etc. (Scotland) Act 2000 (Register of Interests) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Ethical Standards in Public Life etc. (Scotland) Act 2000 (Register of Interests) Regulations 2003 (“the principal regulations”).

items.title: The Pollution Prevention and Control (Designation of Waste Incineration Directive) (Scotland) Order 2003 (revoked)
items.description: This Order designates Council Directive 99/31/EC on the incineration of waste (“the Waste Incineration Directive”) as a relevant directive for the purposes of paragraph 20(2)(c) of Schedule 1 to the Pollution Prevention and Control Act 1999 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Regulation of Care (Scotland) Act 2001 (Commencement No. 3 and Transitional Provisions) Order 2003
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of the Regulation of Care (Scotland) Act 2001 (“the Act”) and makes transitional provisions.

items.title: The Environment Act 1995 (Commencement No. 21) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st April 2003 section 120(1) of the Environment Act 1995, insofar as it relates to paragraphs 29(21) and (22) of Schedule 22 (insofar as those provisions are not already in force) and paragraph 88 of that Schedule for the purposes only of the Landfill (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (“the 2003 Regulations”). Paragraphs 29(21) and (22) make amendments to the Control of Pollution Act 1974 (c. 40) concerning operations to remedy or forestall pollution of water and notices requiring persons to carry out anti-pollution operations. Paragraph 88 amends section 75 of the Environmental Protection Act 1990 (“the 1990 Act”) (meaning of “waste” and household, commercial and industrial waste and special waste). Subsection (2) is replaced, subsection (3) is deleted and new subsections (10) to (12) are inserted. The amended definition of “waste” in the 1990 Act is incorporated in the definition of “waste” in regulation 2 of the 2003 Regulations.

items.title: The Access to Justice Act 1999 (Commencement No. 9 and Transitional Provisions) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order brings into force on 31st March 2003, in relation to Scotland, a number of provisions of the Access to Justice Act 1999 (“the 1999 Act”).

items.title: The Landfill (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations set out a pollution control regime for landfills for the purpose of implementing Council Directive 99/31/EC on the landfill of waste (“the Landfill Directive”) in Scotland. Landfills have previously been subject to either the Waste Management Licensing Regulations 1994 or the Pollution Prevention and Control (Scotland) Regulations 2000 (“the 2000 Regulations”) (which in turn implemented Council Directive 96/61/EC concerning integrated pollution prevention and control (“the IPPC Directive”)).

items.title: The SFGS Farmland Premium Scheme 2003
items.description: This Scheme extends to Scotland only. It provides for the payment of annual grants to abate financial losses incurred in consequence of the conversion of agricultural land (including common grazings) to use for woodlands. The Scheme complies with Council Regulation (EC) No. 1257/99 on support for rural development from the European Agricultural Guidance and Guarantee Fund (EAAGF).

items.title: The Police and Police (Special Constables) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police (Scotland) Regulations 1976 (“the 1976 Regulations”) and the Police (Special Constables) (Scotland) Regulations 1966 (“the 1966 Regulations”) in relation to the qualifications for appointment as a constable or special constable.

items.title: The Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 (Commencement No. 6, Transitional Provisions and Savings) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order brings into force, on 24th March 2003, the following provisions of the Proceeds of Crime Act (“the Act”):–

items.title: The Lerwick Harbour Revision (Constitution) Order 2003
items.description: This Order reconstitutes the Lerwick Port Authority in line with the recommendations of the Trust Ports Review, published by the Department of Environment Transport and the Regions in January 2000 and endorsed by the Scottish Executive.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Vehicle Emissions) (Fixed Penalty) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: The Road Traffic (Vehicle Emissions) (Fixed Penalty) Regulations 1997 (“the 1997 Regulations”) empower certain local authorities in England, Scotland and Wales (“participating authorities”) to issue fixed penalty notices to users of vehicles within their areas who contravene or fail to comply with regulation 61, 61A or 98 of the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”). Those regulations are concerned with emissions of smoke, vapour, gases, oily substances, etc.

items.title: The National Health Service (Constitution of Health Boards) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2003
items.description: This Order deletes article 5 of the National Health Service (Constitution of Health Boards) (Scotland) Order 1974 (“the 1974 Order”) which provided that each health board shall consist of a chairman and such numbers of other members, not more than a maximum number.

items.title: The National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations revoke and restate the provisions in the National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003 (S.S.I. 2003/200) (“the First Amendment Regulations”), which contained errors.

items.title: The Public Appointments and Public Bodies etc. (Scotland) Act 2003 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2003
items.description: The Public Appointments and Public Bodies etc. (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the Act”) received Royal Assent on 11th March 2003. Sections 18, 19, 20 and 21 came into force on Royal Assent.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 13) (Scotland) Order 2002 Partial Revocation Order 2003
items.description: This version of the The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 13) (Scotland) Order 2002 Partial Revocation Order 2003 is the published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer for Scotland. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Police (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police (Scotland) Regulations 1976 (“the 1976 Regulations”) in relation to hours of duty, allowances and expenses. The Regulations come into force on 1st April 2003.

items.title: The Pollution Prevention and Control (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Pollution Prevention and Control (Scotland) Regulations 2000 (S.S.I. 2000/323) (“the 2000 Regulations”), which have already been amended by the Pollution Prevention and Control (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003 (S.S.I. 2003/146) (“the 2003 Regulations”). Regulation 4 of the 2003 Regulations amended regulation 6 of the 2000 Regulations but omitted a reference in the amended regulation 6(2)(a) to paragraph 1 of Schedule 3 to the 2000 Regulations. These Regulations correct that omission.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 2) (Proceeds of Crime Act 2002) 2003
items.description: This Act of Sederunt inserts a new Chapter (Chapter 76A) into the Court of Session Rules 1994 (S.I. 1994/1443) to make provision for applications in the Court of Session in respect of the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 (“the Act of 2002”).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 3) (Applications under the Nationality, Immigration and Asylum Act 2002) 2003
items.description: This Act of Sederunt inserts a new Part (Part XI) into the Court of Session Rules 1994 (S.I. 1994/1443) to make provision for applications for review of a decision of the Immigration Appeal Tribunal in the Court of Session under section 101(2) of the Nationality, Immigration and Asylum Act 2002 (“the Act of 2002”). Part XI provides for applications to be made by application notice to the Outer House in the Form set out in the Schedule inserted by paragraph 2(3) of this Act of Sederunt. The notice must be lodged within 14 days of the Tribunal’s decision and certain documents must be lodged along with the application notice as set out in rule 41.47(3). Applications will be determined by a single judge in the Outer House without a hearing and by reference to written submissions only. The rules also make provision for service of the application notice and service of the court’s order. The expenses of the application may be reserved by the court to be determined by the Tribunal.

items.title: The Plant Health (Great Britain) Amendment (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order amends the Plant Health (Great Britain) Order 1993 (“the principal Order”), in relation to Scotland, so as to implement–

items.title: The Products of Animal Origin (Third Country Imports) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Products of Animal Origin (Third Country Imports) (Scotland) Regulations 2002 (S.S.I. 2002/445 as amended by S.S.I 2002/565 and S.S.I 2003/165) (“the principal Regulations”) which implement Council Directive 97/78/EC laying down the principles governing the organisation of veterinary checks on products entering the Community from third countries (O.J. No. L 24, 30.1.98, p.9).

items.title: The Adults with Incapacity (Management of Residents' Finances) (Scotland) Revocation Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations revoke, prior to their coming into force, the Adults with Incapacity (Management of Residents' Finances) (Scotland) Regulations 2003.

items.title: The Adults with Incapacity (Scotland) Act 2000 (Commencement No. 3) Partial Revocation Order 2003
items.description: This Order revokes article 2(2) of the Adults with Incapacity (Scotland) Act 2000 (Commencement No. 3) Order 2003. Article 2(2) would otherwise have brought into force on 1st April 2003 Part 4 of, and schedule 1 to, the Adults with Incapacity (Scotland) Act 2000, together with certain provisions from schedules 4 to 6 to that Act.

items.title: The Disease Control (Interim Measures) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2003
items.description: This Order amends the Disease Control (Interim Measures) (Scotland) Order 2002 (“the principal Order”). It reduces the 20 day standstill period on the movement of animals contained in articles 3, 4 and 5 of the principal Order to a 13 day period. In addition, consequential amendments are required to articles 4A and 5(5), and there is a need for a consequential revocation to article 2(2)(b) of the Disease Control and Animal Movements (Interim Measures) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2002.

items.title: The Pet Travel Scheme (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which extends to Scotland only, creates, in limited circumstances, a Pet Travel Scheme similar to that already in place in England. The Scheme provides for exemptions from the requirement that pet cats and pet dogs imported into Scotland must be placed in quarantine under the Rabies (Importation of Dogs, Cats and Other Mammals) Order 1974, S.I. 1974/2211.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 12) (Scotland) Order 2002 Revocation Order 2003
items.description: This version of the The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 12) (Scotland) Order 2002 Revocation Order 2003 is the published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer for Scotland. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Kyle of Sutherland Salmon Fishery District (Baits and Lures) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations specify certain baits and lures for the purposes of the definition of “rod and line” in section 24(1) of the Salmon and Freshwater Fisheries (Protection) (Scotland) Act 1951. The Regulations apply to specified rivers in the Kyle of Sutherland Salmon Fishery District (regulation 3).

items.title: The Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 (Exclusions and Exceptions) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order consolidates the Orders (S.I. 1975/1023, 1986/1249 and 1986/2268) that exclude and make exceptions to the provisions of section 4 (effect of rehabilitation) of the Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974. It excludes further proceedings and types of work from certain provisions within that section and updates the terms used to reflect recent Scottish legislation, including the Regulation of Care (Scotland) Act 2001 and the Protection of Children (Scotland) Act 2003.

items.title: The General Commissioners of Income Tax (Expenses) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: The Taxes Management Act 1970 (“the 1970 Act”) provides in section 2A(1) a general rule that an order to pay expenses may not be made against a General Commissioner of income tax in any proceedings in respect of an act or omission in execution, or purported execution, of his or her duty.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees in the National Archives of Scotland) 2003
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes provision for an increase in fees chargeable by National Archive of Scotland, formerly known as the Scottish Record Office.

items.title: The Landfill (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations set out a pollution control regime for landfills for the purpose of implementing Council Directive 99/31/EC on the landfill of waste (“the Landfill Directive”) in Scotland. Landfills have previously been subject to either the Waste Management Licensing Regulations 1994 or the Pollution Prevention and Control (Scotland) Regulations 2000 (“the 2000 Regulations”) (which in turn implemented Council Directive 96/61/EC concerning integrated pollution prevention and control (“the IPPC Directive”)).

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 14) (Scotland) Order 2002 Revocation Order 2003
items.description: This version of the The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 14) (Scotland) Order 2002 Revocation Order 2003 is the published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer for Scotland. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Scottish Public Services Ombudsman Act 2002 (Consequential Modification of Instruments) Order 2003
items.description: This order makes provision consequential on the creation of the office of Scottish Public Services Ombudsman.

items.title: The Community Care (Direct Payments) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations are made under section 12B(1)(b), (1B), (3), (4) and (6) of the Social Work (Scotland) Act 1968 (“the 1968 Act”). Section 12B was originally inserted into the 1968 Act by section 4 of the Community Care (Direct Payments) Act 1996. Section 12B was amended by section 7 of the Community Care and Health (Scotland) Act 2002 (which comes into force on 1st June 2003).

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 2) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Shorthand Writers in the Sheriff Court) (Amendment) 2003
items.description: This Act of Sederunt increases the fees payable to shorthand writers in the Sheriff Court by about 2.6%. The last increase was effected by the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Witnesses and Shorthand Writers in the Sheriff Court) (Amendment) 2002 (S.S.I. 2002/280).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 4) (Fees of Shorthand Writers) 2003
items.description: This Act of Sederunt increases the fees payable to shorthand writers in the Court of Session by about 2.6%. The last increase was effected by the Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 4) (Fees of Solicitors, Shorthand Writers and Witnesses) 2002 (S.S.I. 2002/301).

items.title: The Agricultural Holdings (Scotland) Act 2003 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2003
items.description: This Order brings into force section 72 of the Agricultural Holdings (Scotland) Act 2003 on 22nd May 2003, with the exception of subsections (2) and (10) of that section. The Act received Royal Assent on 22nd April 2003.

items.title: The Criminal Legal Aid (Youth Courts) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Criminal Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Regulations 1989 (“the 1989 Regulations”), the Criminal Legal Aid (Scotland) Regulations 1996 (“the 1996 Regulations”) and the Criminal Legal Aid (Fixed Payments) (Scotland) Regulations 1999 (“the 1999 Regulations”), so as to provide that–

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Ordinary Cause Rules) Amendment (Form of Simplified Divorce Application) 2003
items.description: This version of the Act of Sederunt (Ordinary Cause Rules) Amendment (Form of Simplified Divorce Application) 2003 is the published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer for Scotland. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Montrose Port Authority Harbour Revision (Constitution) Order 2003
items.description: This Order reconstitutes the Montrose Port Authority. It provides for the appointment of members of the Authority with experience in relevant matters, makes other provision with respect to the Authority and amends the borrowing powers of the Authority.

items.title: The Lanarkshire Primary Care National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2003
items.description: The Lanarkshire Primary Care National Health Service Trust, which was established by the Lanarkshire Primary Care National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1998 (S.I. 1998/2723), as amended, has the power to provide goods and services for the purpose of the Health Service at, from or through a number of hospitals named in the Order. The purpose of this Order is to add Stonehouse Hospital to those hospitals named.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Ordinary Cause, Summary Application, Summary Cause and Small Claim Rules) Amendment (Miscellaneous) 2003
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes miscellaneous amendments to the rules of procedure in the sheriff court. It amends the Ordinary Cause Rules, the Summary Applications, Statutory Applications and Appeals etc. Rules, the Summary Cause Rules, and the Small Claim Rules.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (Orkney) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxins which cause amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Summary Applications, Statutory Applications and Appeals etc. Rules) Amendment (Immigration and Asylum) 2003
items.description: This Act of Sederunt further amends the Act of Sederunt (Summary Applications, Statutory Applications and Appeals etc. Rules) 1999 (“the 1999 Rules”).

items.title: The Tobacco Advertising and Promotion (Sponsorship Transitional Provisions) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Tobacco Advertising and Promotion (Sponsorship Transitional Provisions) (Scotland) Regulations 2003 in order to clarify the conditions under which there is a time limited exception for sponsorship of exceptional global events. “Exceptional global event” is defined in regulation 2 of those Regulations to mean an event or activity that takes place in at least two continents and three sovereign states. The clarification makes it clear that the reference to “advertisements” in the conditions which apply to the exception is a reference to tobacco advertisements.

items.title: The Adults with Incapacity (Management of Residents' Finances) (No. 2) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations make provisions in relation to the management of the affairs of certain adults with incapacity in accordance with Part 4 (management of residents' finances) of the Adults with Incapacity (Scotland) Act 2000 (asp 4) (“the Act”).

items.title: The Adults with Incapacity (Scotland) Act 2000 (Commencement No. 4) Order 2003
items.description: This Order appoints 29th May 2003 as the date for the coming into force of sections 37(2) and (9), 39(3) and 41(d) of the Adults with Incapacity (Scotland) Act 2000, but only for the purposes of making Regulations. They will come fully into force on 1st October 2003.

items.title: The Litter (Fixed Penalty) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: Section 88 of the Environmental Protection Act 1990 provides for the payment of a fixed penalty to discharge any liability to conviction for the offence of leaving litter under section 87 of that Act.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Summary Applications, Statutory Applications and Appeals etc. Rules) Amendment (International Criminal Court) 2003
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Summary Applications, Statutory Applications and Appeals etc. Rules) 1999. The amendments are in consequence of the International Criminal Court (Scotland) Act 2001. A new Part XX sets out the procedure for–

items.title: The National Health Service Superannuation Scheme (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service Superannuation Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 1995 (“the 1995 Regulations”) which provide for the superannuation of persons engaged in the National Health Service in Scotland. Certain provisions have retrospective effect as authorised by section 12 of the Superannuation Act 1972: regulations 3 and 6 shall have effect from 1st April 2003 and Regulations 8, 9, 10 and 11 shall have effect from 1st April 2002. All other amendments shall have effect from the date of coming into force of these Regulations.

items.title: The Urban Waste Water Treatment (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Urban Waste Water Treatment (Scotland) Regulations 1994, (“the principal Regulations”) which implement Council Directive 91/271/EEC concerning urban waste water treatment (“the Directive”). The principal Regulations, amongst other matters, require the Scottish Ministers to keep under review the identification of “sensitive areas” and “high natural dispersion areas” which are areas of water defined in accordance with specified criteria. The functions of the Secretary of State in respect of the Directive were transferred to the Scottish Ministers by section 53 of the Scotland Act 1998 (c. 46).

items.title: The Sweeteners in Food Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, further amend the Sweeteners in Food Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/3123, as already amended) (“the 1995 Regulations”) by–

items.title: The Feeding Stuffs (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, amend the Feeding Stuffs (Sampling and Analysis) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/1663, as already amended), the Feeding Stuffs (Enforcement) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/2325, as already amended) and the Feeding Stuffs (Establishments and Intermediaries) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/1872, as already amended).

items.title: The Food Supplements (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations implement Directive 2002/46/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on the approximation of the laws of the Member States relating to food supplements (“the Directive”).

items.title: The Ethical Standards in Public Life etc. (Scotland) Act 2000 (Devolved Public Bodies) (No. 2) Order 2003
items.description: This Order modifies schedule 3 to the Ethical Standards in Public Life etc. (Scotland) Act 2000, which lists those bodies that are a “devolved public body” for the purpose of that Act.

items.title: The St Mary’s Music School (Aided Places) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: The Regulations amend the St Mary’s Music School (Aided Places) (Scotland) Regulations 2001 to uprate, with effect from 1st August 2003, the qualifying income levels for the remission of fees and charges and the making of grants under the aided places scheme, and to add receipt of the Child Tax Credit as one of the qualifying benefits for remission of charges for meals for day pupils.

items.title: The Education (Assisted Places) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Assisted Places) (Scotland) Regulations 2001 to uprate the qualifying income levels for the remission of fees and charges and the making of grants under the assisted places scheme and to add receipt of the Child Tax Credit as one of the qualifying benefits for remission of charges for school meals.

items.title: The Road User Charging (Classes of Motor Vehicles) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations relate to the provisions of Part 3 of the Transport (Scotland) Act 2001 which enable road user charging schemes (“charging schemes”) to be made.

items.title: The Agricultural Wages (Scotland) Act 1949 Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Agricultural Wages (Scotland) Act 1949 (“the 1949 Act”).

items.title: The Home Energy Efficiency Scheme Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: The Home Energy Efficiency Scheme Regulations 1997 (S.I. 1997/790, as amended) provide for the making of grants for the improvement of energy efficiency in dwellings occupied by persons on low income or elderly persons. These Regulations amend those 1997 Regulations as regards Scotland only.

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st August 2003, amend the Education (Student Loans) Regulations 1998 governing loans made under the Education (Student Loans) Act 1990. These are mortgage style repayment loans made, for the most part, to students who began their courses before 1st August 1998.

items.title: The Accountability of Local Authorities (Publication of Information about Finance and Performance) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations make provision in relation to the publication by local authorities of information about finance and performance. Section 13 of the Local Government in Scotland Act 2003 (“the 2003 Act”) places local authorities under a general duty to make arrangements for the reporting to the public of the performance of their functions.

items.title: The Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 2003 (Saving and Transitional Provisions) Order 2003
items.description: This Order should be read with the Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 2003 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2003 (S.S.I. 2003/288), which brings certain provisions of the Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 2003 into force on 27th June 2003. Those provisions include sections 27, 28, 38 and 54 of that Act.

items.title: The Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 2003 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2003
items.description: This Order brings into force on 27th June 2003 the provisions of the Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 2003 specified in the Schedule to the Order. The Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 2003 (Saving and Transitional Provisions) Order 2003 (S.S.I. 2003/287) sets out separate transitional and savings provisions in relation to the commencement of sections 27, 28, 38 and 54 of that Act.

items.title: The Contaminants in Food (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, revoke and re-enact with changes the Contaminants in Food (Scotland) Regulations 2002 (as amended).

items.title: The Drugs Courts (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order prescribes that there shall be a drugs court within the Sheriffdoms of Glasgow and Strathkelvin and Tayside, Central and Fife (article 2 and the Schedule). A “drugs court” is defined in section 42(1) of the Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 2003 as a court especially appropriate to deal with cases involving persons dependent on, or with a propensity to misuse, drugs. In terms of section 42(2)(a) of that Act, the sheriff principal is to nominate a court within the sheriffdom to be a drugs court.

items.title: The Cocoa and Chocolate Products (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland, implement Directive 2000/36/EC of the European Parliament and the Council relating to cocoa and chocolate products intended for human consumption . They revoke and replace the Cocoa and Chocolate Products (Scotland) Regulations 1976, as amended.

items.title: The Road User Charging (Consultation and Publication) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations relate to Part 3 of the Transport (Scotland) Act 2001 which enable road user charging schemes (“charging schemes”) to be made.

items.title: The Fruit Juices and Fruit Nectars (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, implement Council Directive 2001/112/EC relating to fruit juices and certain similar products intended for human consumption (O.J. No. L 10, 12.1.2002, p.58). They revoke and replace the Fruit Juices and Fruit Nectars (Scotland) Regulations 1977 (S.I. 1977/1026), as amended.

items.title: The Agricultural Holdings (Relevant Date and Relevant Period) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order sets the relevant date and the length of the relevant period for the purposes of Part 6 of the Agricultural Holdings (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the Act”).

items.title: The National Health Service (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) (Scotland) Regulations 2001 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the charges for drugs and appliances provided under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The National Health Service (Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1995 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the provision of pharmaceutical services under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The Stevenson College (Change of Name) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order changes the name of Stevenson College to Stevenson College Edinburgh. That college is under the control of a board of management in terms of the Further and Higher Education (Scotland) Act 1992 and the Order therefore also changes the name of the board of management to reflect the new name of the College.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical Services Supplementary Lists) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Medical Services Supplementary Lists) (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (“the principal Regulations”) which made provision for the preparation and keeping, by Health Boards, of lists of medical practitioners approved to assist in the provision of general medical services.

items.title: The Collagen and Gelatine (Intra-Community Trade) (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, implement Commission Decision 2003/42/EC (O.J. No. L 13, 18.1.03, p.24) – “the Commission Decision” –so far as it relates to trade between Member States of the European Community and imposes new or changed obligations on the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Restriction on Days at Sea) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Order 2003
items.description: This Order amends the Sea Fishing (Restriction on Days at Sea) (Scotland) Order 2003 (“the principal Order”), which provides for the enforcement in Scotland of the provisions of Annex XVII (“the Annex”) to Council Regulation 2341/2002 (O.J. No. L 356, 31.12.02, p.12) fixing for certain fish stocks and groups of fish stocks the fishing opportunities in Community waters for 2003.

items.title: The Cremation (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, amend the Cremation (Scotland) Regulations 1935 (“the principal Regulations”), making provision concerning the cremation of parts of the body of a deceased person, those parts having been removed in the course of a post-mortem examination.

items.title: The Agricultural Subsidies (Appeals) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Agricultural Subsidies (Appeals) (Scotland) Regulations 2000 (“the principal Regulations”) to add to the list of decisions amenable to review and appeal set out in regulation 4 of the principal Regulations, certain decisions made under provisions of the SFGS Farmland Premium Scheme 2003 (regulation 2). That scheme is funded in part from European Community Funds.

items.title: The Rural Stewardship Scheme (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Rural Stewardship Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2001 (“the principal Regulations”), which implement Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1750/1999 (O.J. No. L 214, 13.8.99, p.31) which prescribes detailed rules for the application of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1257/1999 (O.J. No. L 160, 26.6.99, p.80) on support for rural development from the European Agricultural Guidance and Guarantee Fund. The principal Regulations extend to Scotland only.

items.title: The Oil and Fibre Plant Seeds Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Oil and Fibre Plants Seeds Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/2007) as amended (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Agricultural Holdings (Scotland) Act 2003 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2003
items.description: This Order brings into force sections 72(10) and 73 of the Agricultural Holdings (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the Act”) on 1st July 2003. The Act received Royal Assent on 22nd April 2003. The remainder of section 72 of the Act (other than subsection (2)) and section 70(7) and (8) of the Act for these purposes were brought into force by the Agricultural Holdings (Scotland) Act 2003 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2003 (S.S.I. 2003/248).

items.title: The National Health Service (Functions of the Common Services Agency) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Order 2003
items.description: This Order further amends the National Health Service (Functions of the Common Services Agency) (Scotland) Order 1974 (“the principal Order”).

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical Services) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Medical Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1995, which regulate the terms on which doctors provide general medical services under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The Condensed Milk and Dried Milk (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland, implement Council Directive 2001/114/EC relating to certain partly or wholly dehydrated preserved milk products for human consumption (O.J. No. L 15, 17.1.02, p.19). They also implement First Commission Directive 79/1067/EEC which lays down Community methods of analysis for testing condensed milk and dried milk products (O.J. No. L 327, 24.12.79, p.29) and First Commission Directive 1987/524/EEC which lays down Community methods of sampling for chemical analysis for the monitoring of preserved milk products (O.J. No. L 306, 28.10.87, p.24). They revoke and replace the Condensed Milk and Dried Milk (Scotland) Regulations 1977, as amended.

items.title: The Feeding Stuffs (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, amend the Feeding Stuffs (Scotland) Regulations 2000 (S.S.I. 2000/453, as already amended).

items.title: The Improvement and Repairs Grant (Prescribed Valuation Band) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: Section 240(2)(c) and (4) of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1987 provides that a local authority shall not approve an application for improvement grant where the value of the premises concerned, expressed in terms of council tax valuation bands, exceeds certain limits prescribed by order. Section 248(5) of the 1987 Act extends these provisions to applications for repairs grant. This Order prescribes a single valuation band as the new limit for the purpose of these provisions and revokes the Housing (Valuation Bands for Improvement and Repairs Grants) (Scotland) Order 1996.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (Scotland) Partial Revocation Order 2003
items.description: This Order is a partial revocation of the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (Scotland) Order 2003 (“the principal Order”) which prohibited–

items.title: The Mental Health (Care and Treatment) (Scotland) Act 2003 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2003
items.description: This Order brings into force paragraph 10 in Part 2 of schedule 1 to the Mental Health (Care and Treatment) (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the Act”). The Act received Royal Assent on 25th April 2003.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Summary Applications, Statutory Applications and Appeals etc. Rules) Amendment (Anti social Behaviour Orders) 2003
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Summary Applications, Statutory Applications and Appeals etc. Rules) 1999 (“the 1999 Rules”).

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (Orkney) (No. 2) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxins which cause amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Argyll and Clyde National Health Service Trusts (Dissolution) Order 2003
items.description: This Order provides by article 2 for the dissolution on 1st July 2003 of the three National Health Service trusts detailed in Schedule 1. As a consequence of those dissolutions this Order also revokes the Orders establishing those trusts and Orders amending those establishment Orders, which are all detailed in Schedule 2.

items.title: The Collagen and Gelatine (Intra-Community Trade) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, amend the Collagen and Gelatine (Intra Community Trade) (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (“the principal Regulations”) by substituting the coming into force date of 30th June 2003 with a new date of 30th September 2003.

items.title: The Budget (Scotland) Act 2003 Amendment Order 2003
items.description: This Order amends the Budget (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the Act”), which makes provision, for Financial Year 2003/04, for the use of resources by the Scottish Administration and certain bodies whose expenditure is payable out of the Scottish Consolidated Fund, for authorising the use of resources, and for limits on the capital expenditure of and borrowing of local authorities and certain other public bodies. In particular, the Order amends the Act so as to–

items.title: The Water Industry (Scotland) Act 2002 (Consequential Provisions) Order 2003
items.description: This Order makes modifications of enactments which appear to be necessary or expedient in consequence of the Water Industry (Scotland) Act 2002 (asp 3) (“the 2002 Act”). Section 20 of the 2002 Act created Scottish Water, which took over the functions previously exercisable by the former water and sewerage authorities (the East, North and West of Scotland Water Authorities).

items.title: The Products of Animal Origin (Third Country Imports) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Products of Animal Origin (Third Country Imports) (Scotland) Regulations 2002 (“the principal Regulations”) which implement Council Directive 97/78/EC laying down the principles governing the organisation of veterinary checks on products entering the Community from third countries (O.J. No. L 24, 30.1.98, p.9).

items.title: The Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) Amendment (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which extends to Scotland only, amends the Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) Order 1978 (“the 1978 Order”). Its effects are to substitute for Schedule 1 to the 1978 Order the Schedule 1 in this Order and to add a Schedule 2 to the 1978 Order (article 2). Schedule 1 provides an updated list of disinfectants approved by the Scottish Ministers. Schedule 2 lists disinfectants which, although no longer approved, can continue to be used as approved disinfectants until 31st August 2003.

items.title: The Form of Repair Notice (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the form of repair notice that is to be served by a local authority when requiring repairs be carried out to a house in a state of serious disrepair and revoke the existing form of notice set out as Form 1 in the Schedule to the Housing (Forms) (Scotland) Regulations 1980.

items.title: The Form of Improvement Order (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the form of the improvement order that is to be used by a local authority for the purpose of section 88(1) of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1987 when requiring works to be carried out to a house not situated within a housing action area to bring it up to the tolerable standard and to put it in a good state of repair.

items.title: The Housing Grants (Form of Cessation or Partial Cessation of Conditions Notice) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the form of notice that is to be used by a local authority where the conditions, or some of the conditions, that apply where the local authority have provided payment of an improvement or repairs grant or a grant for means of escape from fire for a house in multiple occupation cease to apply.

items.title: The Housing Grants (Form of Notice of Payment) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the form of the notice that a local authority is required to register in the Land Register or the General Register of Sasines in respect of payment of an improvement or repairs grant, or a grant for fire escapes for a house in multiple occupation.

items.title: The Tobacco Advertising and Promotion (Sponsorship Transitional Provisions) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, provide for certain transitional exceptions to the prohibition, under section 10 of the Tobacco Advertising and Promoting Act 2002 (“the Act”) on sponsorship agreements whose purpose or effect is to promote a tobacco product.

items.title: The Environmental Impact Assessment (Water Management) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations further implement, in relation to water management projects for agriculture including irrigation and land drainage projects in Scotland, Council Directive 85/337/EEC on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment (O.J. No. L 175, 5.7.1985, p.40), as amended by Council Directive 97/11/EC (O.J. No. L 73, 14.3.1997, p.5) (“the Directive”).

items.title: The Landfill (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These regulations amend the Landfill (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (the “principal Regulations”).

items.title: The National Health Service (Compensation for Premature Retirement) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations consolidate, with minor amendments, the provisions of the National Health Service (Compensation for Premature Retirement) (Scotland) Regulations 1981. They provide for the payment of compensation to or in respect of a person who was employed in the National Health Service in Scotland and has prematurely retired from that employment by reason of redundancy or in the interests of the efficiency of the service.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Summary Applications, Statutory Applications and Appeals etc. Rules) Amendment (Standards Commission for Scotland) 2003
items.description: This Act of Sederunt further amends the Act of Sederunt (Summary Applications, Statutory Applications and Appeals etc. Rules) 1999 (“the 1999 Rules”).

items.title: The Public Appointments and Public Bodies etc. (Scotland) Act 2003 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2003
items.description: The Public Appointments and Public Bodies etc. (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the Act”) received Royal Assent on 11th March 2003. Sections 18, 19, 20 and 21 came into force on Royal Assent.

items.title: The Education (School Meals) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations prescribe, for the purposes of section 53(3)(a)(iv) and (b)(iii) of the Education (Scotland) Act 1980 (“the 1980 Act”) the tax credit entitling a pupil to free school meals. Where the pupil’s parent or the pupil is in receipt of Child Tax Credit, based on an annual income not exceeding the amount determined for the purposes of section 7(1)(a) of the Tax Credits Act 2002, currently £13,230 per annum, and is not in receipt of Working Tax Credit, the pupil is entitled to free school meals.

items.title: The Movement of Animals (Restrictions) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order revokes and re-enacts the provisions of the Diseases of Animals (Ascertainment of Disease) Order 1985 (S.I. 1985/1765) and the Movement of Animals (Restrictions) Order 1990 (S.I. 1990/760), so far as they apply to Scotland.

items.title: The Diseases of Poultry (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order (read with the Animal Health Act 1981) further implements Council Directive 92/40/EEC introducing Community measures for the control of avian influenza (O.J. No. L 167, 22.6.92, p.1) and Council Directive 92/66/EEC introducing Community measures for the control of Newcastle disease (O.J. No. L 260, 5.9.92, p.1) (“the Directives”). The Directives were previously implemented by the Diseases of Poultry Order 1994 (“the 1994 Order”) and the Diseases of Poultry (Amendment) Order 1997, both revoked by this Order. This Order, like the Directives, now applies to ratites.

items.title: The Greenburn Light Railway (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This version of the The Greenburn Light Railway (Scotland) Order 2003 is the published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer for Scotland. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 3) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (East Coast) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (East Coast) (No. 2) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Prohibition of Fishing for Scallops (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of the law of Scotland only, introduces measures for the conservation of king scallops.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 4) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Radioactivity in Sheep) Partial Revocation (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, is a partial revocation of the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Radioactivity in Sheep) Order 1991.

items.title: The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Scotland) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations provide in respect of persons specified in the regulations, for the remission or repayment of the whole or part of certain charges which would otherwise be payable by those persons under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 (“the Act”) and the National Health Service (Primary Care) Act 1997 (“the 1997 Act”). They also provide in respect of persons specified in the regulations, for the payment, as set out, of certain travelling expenses, including those of the person’s companion, and certain costs of overnight accommodation for the person and their companion.

items.title: The Sexual Offences (Amendment) Act 2000 (Commencement No. 4) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order brings into force on 11th August 2003 to the extent, if any, that they are not already in force sections 1(3) and (5), 2(4), 3, 4(1), (2), 3 (except paragraphs (a) and (b)), (4) (except paragraph (d)), (5), (6), (7), (8) and (9) (except those provisions in subsection (9) which relate only to England and Wales or Northern Ireland), 5(1) and (3) and 6(2) of the Sexual Offences (Amendment) Act 2000 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (East Coast) (No. 3) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 5) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Food (Hot Chilli and Hot Chilli Products) (Emergency Control) (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement Commission Decision 2003/460/EC on emergency measures regarding hot chilli and hot chilli products (O.J. No. L 154, 21.6.03, p.114) as corrected by a corrigendum published on 25th July 2003 (O.J. No. L 186, 25.7.03, p.47).

items.title: The Public Appointments and Public Bodies etc. (Scotland) Act 2003 (Commencement No. 3) Order 2003
items.description: This Order brings into force on 15th August 2003 certain provisions of the Public Appointments and Public Bodies etc. (Scotland) Act 2003 which relate to the dissolution of the Scottish Conveyancing and Executry Services Board (“the Board”). These include provisions relating to the transfer of functions from the Board to the Law Society of Scotland and the Scottish Solicitors' Discipline Tribunal and conferring on conveyancing and executry practitioners certain notarial and other functions.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No.5) (Insolvency Proceedings) 2003
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes changes to Chapter 74 of the Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session) 1994 to support changes made to the Insolvency (Scotland) Rules 1986 by the Insolvency (Scotland) Amendment Rules 2003, and changes made to the Insolvency (Scotland) Act 1986 by the Enterprise Act 2002.

items.title: Act of Adjournal (Criminal Appeals) 2003
items.description: This Act of Adjournal makes provision for the lodging of written submissions in appeals against sentence in solemn and summary proceedings.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Sheriff Court Company Insolvency Rules 1986) Amendment 2003
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Sheriff Court Company Insolvency Rules) 1986 (“the original rules”).

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 6) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (East Coast) (No. 4) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (East Coast) (No. 5) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Food (Brazil Nuts) (Emergency Control) (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations are made under section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972 in consequence of it appearing that the importation into Scotland of Brazil nuts in shell originating in, or consigned from, Brazil, may involve imminent risk of injury to health.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 7) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Nursing and Midwifery Student Allowances (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st October 2003, amend the Nursing and Midwifery Student Allowances (Scotland) Regulations 1992 (“the principal Regulations”). The principal Regulations govern allowances paid to persons attending courses of nursing and midwifery education leading to registration with the Nursing and Midwifery Council, and prescribe the conditions and requirements subject to which allowances may be paid.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 8) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Gaming Act (Variation of Fees) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which extends to Scotland only, increases the fees to be charged in relation to the grant, renewal and transfer of gaming licences in Scotland under the Gaming Act 1968 (article 2 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Inshore Fishing (Prohibition of Fishing and Fishing Methods) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2003
items.description: This Order amends the Inshore Fishing (Prohibition of Fishing and Fishing Methods) (Scotland) Order 1989 by–

items.title: The Collagen and Gelatine (Intra-Community Trade) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, amend the Collagen and Gelatine (Intra Community Trade) (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (“the principal Regulations”). The principal Regulations gave effect to Commission Decision 2003/42/EC (O.J. No. L 13, 18.1.03, p.24). That Decision was subsequently amended by Commission Decision 2003/503/EC (O.J. No. L 170, 9.7.03, p.30). That subsequent Decision delayed the date of application of Commission Decision 2003/42/EC from 30th June 2003 to 30th September 2003.

items.title: The Police Pensions (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the following Regulations, as they have effect in Scotland: the Police Pensions Regulations 1987 (“the 1987 Regulations”), which provide for the superannuation of members of police forces, and the Police Pensions (Additional Voluntary Contributions) Regulations 1991 (“the AVC Regulations”), which make provision for the payment of additional voluntary contributions by police officers to secure additional pension benefits.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 9) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) (West Coast) (No. 10) (Scotland) Order 2003
items.description: This Order, which forms part of Scots law only, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food rendered unsuitable for that purpose by virtue of shellfish having been affected by the toxin which causes amnesic shellfish poisoning in human beings.

items.title: The Animal By-Products (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations make provision in Scotland for the administration and enforcement of the  “Community Regulation”, Regulation (EC) No 1774/2002 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 3 October 2002 laying down health rules concerning animal by-products not intended for human consumption (O.J. No. L 273, 10.10.2002, p.1.). They restrict what may be done in relation to such products and come into force on 1st October 2003.

items.title: The Food (Figs, Hazelnuts and Pistachios from Turkey) (Emergency Control) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, amend the Food (Figs, Hazelnuts and Pistachios from Turkey) (Emergency Control) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2002 (S.S.I. 2002/424) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Food (Pistachios from Iran) (Emergency Control) (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description:
