items.description: These Regulations further amend the Care Homes (Wales) Regulations 2002. Regulation 19 of those Regulations requires certain information and documents to be available in respect of persons who work at a care home, including criminal record certificates issued under the Police Act 1997. In relation to staff employed at a care home immediately before 1st April 2002, the requirement in respect of criminal record certificates does not apply until 1st April 2003. This requirement is amended by regulation 2(3) of these Regulations so that it does not apply until 31st October 2004.

items.title: The National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) and (General Ophthalmic Services) (Amendment) (Wales) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) Regulations 1997 (“the 1997 Regulations”) and the National Health Service (General Ophthalmic Services) Regulations 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”).

items.title: The National Assistance (Residential Accommodation) (Disregarding of Resources) (Wales) Regulations 2003
items.description: The Health and Social Care Act 2001 (c. 15) amended section 21 of the National Assistance Act 1948 (c. 29) by substituting subsections (2A) and (2B). Section 21 provides for residential accommodation arranged by local authorities for expectant and nursing mothers and other persons who by reason of age, illness, disability or any other circumstances are in need of care and attention which is not otherwise available to them. The new subsections provide that regulations may specify or provide for the determination of a person’s resources which the local authority shall disregard when deciding for the purposes of section 21(1)(a) or (aa) whether or not the person needs care and attention which is not otherwise available to him or her.

items.title: The Administration of the Rent Officer Service (Wales) Order 2003
items.description: This Order provides, in accordance with powers exercisable by the National Assembly for Wales (“the National Assembly”) under section 64B of the Rent Act 1977, that the appointment, remuneration and administration of rent officers in Wales are to be functions of the National Assembly for Wales rather than of local authorities.

items.title: The Preserved Counties (Amendment to Boundaries) (Wales) Order 2003
items.description: The Local Government Act 1972 as amended by The Local Government (Wales) Act 1994 retains the former 8 County Council areas as “Preserved Counties” with, in some cases, modified boundaries. These operate for limited purposes concerning such matters as Lord Lieutenants and Sheriffs.

items.title: The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Amendment) (Wales) Regulations 2003 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) Regulations 1988 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for the remission of National Health Service charges and for the payment of travelling expenses in certain cases.

items.title: The Products of Animal Origin (Third Country Imports) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Products of Animal Origin (Third Country Imports) (Wales) Regulations 2002 (S.I. 2002/1387 (W.136), as amended by S.I. 2002/3011 (W.282) and S.I. 2002/3239 (W.307)) ('the principal Regulations') which implement Council Directive 97/78/EC laying down the principles governing the organisation of veterinary checks on products entering the Community from third countries (OJ No. L24, 30.1.98, p.9).

items.title: The Feeding Stuffs (Amendment) (Wales) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Feeding Stuffs Regulations 2000 (S.I. 2000/2481, as already amended) which in so far as they relate to Wales, the Feeding Stuffs (Enforcement) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/2325, as already amended) in so far as they relate to Wales, and the Feeding Stuffs (Establishments and Intermediaries) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/1872, as already amended) in so far as they relate to Wales.

items.title: The Leasehold Reform (Collective Enfranchisement) (Counter-notices) (Wales) Regulations 2003
items.description: A person who receives a notice for a claim for collective enfranchisement made under Part I of the Leasehold Reform, Housing and Urban Development Act 1993 (“the 1993 Act”) must respond by giving a counter-notice admitting or opposing the claim. These Regulations prescribe a requirement, in addition to those specified in section 21 of the 1993 Act, as regards the contents of the counter-notice.

items.title: The Leasehold Reform (Notices) (Amendment) (Wales) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations amend the Leasehold Reform (Notices) Regulations 1997 and provide a new form to be used by landlords replying to claims for enfranchisement and extension of long leaseholds under the Leasehold Reform Act 1967 (“the 1967 Act”). Provisions of the 1967 Act which are relevant to the Form in the Schedule to these Regulations have been amended by the Commonhold and Leasehold Reform Act 2002 as mentioned in the Notes to the Form. The new Form is to be used for notices in reply given on or after the date these Regulations come into force. Forms which are substantially to the same effect as those prescribed may also be used.

items.title: The Zoo Licensing Act 1981 (Amendment) (Wales) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations contain provisions implementing Council Directive 1999/22/EC relating to the keeping of wild animals in zoos in Wales (O.J. No. L094, 9.4.1999, p 24-26) (“the Directive”). The Zoo Licensing Act 1981 (Amendment) (England and Wales) Regulations 2002 (the “2002 Regulations”) inserted a new section 22A in the Zoo Licensing Act 1981 (“the Act”) with the effect that the other amendments in those regulations applied only to England. By virtue of these Regulations section 22A ceases to have effect, thereby applying to Wales the amendments made by the 2002 Regulations.

items.title: Commission For Health Improvement (Functions) (Wales) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations make provision in relation to the functions of the Commission for Health Improvement established under section 19 of the Health Act 1999 (“the Commission”).

items.title: The Commons Registration (General) (Amendment) (Wales) Regulations 2003
items.description: These Regulations provide, in relation to Wales, for the increase of fees prescribed in the Commons Registration (General) Regulations 1966 (S.I. 1966/1471), which were previously increased by the Commons Registration (General) (Amendment) Regulations 1980 (S.I. 1980/1195) and the Commons Registration (General) (Amendment) Regulations 1989 (S.I. 1989/2167)), for searches of the Registers of Common Land and Town and Village Greens and for providing certified copies of entries in the Registers.
